urgency.message: 86 critical CVE(s) and 3 new KEV addition(s) in the last 7 days. Review immediately.

items.title: Building more resilient CNI: what industry pen testers told us
items.summary: Pen testers suggest what organisations can do to make their job more difficult.

items.title: Martin Lee: Running through the Arctic (and the threat landscape)
items.summary: Ever wonder how someone goes from studying human viruses to leading cybersecurity teams? In this Humans of Talos, we’re joined by Martin Lee, EMEA Lead, to talk about his journey into the industry.

items.title: ARToken: Inside an EvilTokens affiliate panel targeting Microsoft 365
items.summary:
Cisco Talos identified a fully-featured phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) operator panel, branded "ARToken," that shares infrastructure, API contracts, and operational patterns with the EvilTokens platform documented by Sekoia and Microsoft in early 2026.
  The ARToken panel exposes 80+ AP

items.title: Frontier AI: Six Questions Every Enterprise Should Ask Security Vendors
items.summary:
From model selection and automation to validation and measurable results, the right questions can help enterprises separate genuine AI capabilities from marketing hype.
The post Frontier AI: Six Questions Every Enterprise Should Ask Security Vendors appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Claude Helped a Hacker Find a Way to Issue Tickets to Almost Every US Music Festival
items.summary: A researcher found that using Anthropic’s Claude Opus 4.7, he could break into the website of Front Gate—used by every festival from Lollapalooza to Bonnaroo—and freely issue any ticket he chose.

items.title: Microsoft Accelerates Quantum-Safe Push with New Timeline
items.summary: Microsoft has brought forward its timelines for transitioning to post-quantum cryptography (PQC)

items.title: Amazon fined $2.25M for withholding evidence from fraud victims
items.summary: The U.S. Federal Trade Commission (FTC) says Amazon will pay a $2.25 million civil penalty to settle charges that it blocked identity theft victims' access to transaction records. [...]

items.title: Apple Patches Dozens of Vulnerabilities Across iOS, macOS, and Safari
items.summary:
The updates fix vulnerabilities in WebKit, the kernel, WebRTC, Web Extensions, and other components affecting iPhone, iPad, Mac, and Safari users.
The post Apple Patches Dozens of Vulnerabilities Across iOS, macOS, and Safari appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Insurance Giant Aflac Discloses Data Breach Impacting Millions
items.summary: Aflac Japan has notified regulators that policy details and personal and banking information have been compromised

items.title: Dawnguard Raises $6.3 Million for Security Architecture Automation Platform
items.summary:
The company has publicly launched its solution to help organizations design, build, and operate secure cloud systems.
The post Dawnguard Raises $6.3 Million for Security Architecture Automation Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Massive Password Spray Campaign Targeting Azure CLI
items.summary:
Hackers were seen making over 81 million login attempts originating from systems associated with hosting provider LSHIY.
The post Massive Password Spray Campaign Targeting Azure CLI appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Adobe patches seven max severity ColdFusion, Campaign flaws
items.summary: Adobe has released security patches for seven maximum-severity vulnerabilities in the ColdFusion web app development platform and the Campaign Classic marketing automation platform. [...]

items.title: Phantom Squatting Uses AI-Hallucinated Domains for Phishing and Malware
items.summary:
Large language models keep inventing web addresses that do not exist. Attackers have started buying those made-up domains before anyone else can, then hosting phishing pages on them to catch traffic that AI tools point their way.

Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42 calls the trick phantom squatti

items.title: Anthropic Restores Claude Fable 5 After U.S. Lifts Jailbreak-Linked Export Controls
items.summary:
Anthropic is putting Claude Fable 5 back online worldwide. On June 30, the U.S. Commerce Department lifted the export controls it had imposed on Fable and its more tightly controlled sibling Mythos 5 about two and a half weeks earlier.

Fable 5 returns to users on Wednesday, July 1, across Clau

items.title: Google Patches 382 Chrome Vulnerabilities
items.summary:
Fifteen of the newly patched flaws have been rated ‘critical’ and 67 have been rated ‘high severity’.
The post Google Patches 382 Chrome Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Risky Business #844 -- China closes AI vulndev gap as USA lifts Fable ban
items.summary:
On this week’s show Patrick Gray, Adam Boileau and James Wilson discuss the week’s cybersecurity news. They cover:


  Anthropic’s Fable 5 returning while OpenAI’s GPT-5.6 gets thrown in model jail
  Distillation, cheap tokens, and AI chat harvesting is an industry in China

items.title: Azure CLI Password Spray Hits at Least 78 Microsoft Accounts in 81M+ Attempts
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have warned of a "massive, ongoing, automated password spray attack" aimed at Microsoft's Azure command-line interface (CLI), compromising dozens of accounts in the process.

The activity, per Huntress, originates from an IPv6 address range (2a0a:d683::/32) controlled by in

items.title: Researcher Analyzes 3,000 Live ClickFix Payloads, Exposing API-Driven Malware Delivery
items.summary:
ClickFix, the trick that fools people into running malware by hand, has quietly grown a back office.

New research shows the malicious commands behind its fake "prove you're human" pages are now handed out by API-driven servers that give each visitor the same malware in a different disguise. The sam

items.title: Why Ask Credentials If There Are Secret Codes?, (Wed, Jul 1st)
items.summary: This morning, an interesting phishing email hit my mailbox. It targets Metamask[1], a cryptocurrency wallet, available as a browser extension and a mobile app, that lets users store, send, and receive crypto money. It&#x27s pretty popular, so a juicy target for criminals. In February, I al

items.title: Citrix Patches Six NetScaler Flaws Allowing File Read and Denial-of-Service
items.summary:
Citrix on Tuesday released security updates to address multiple flaws in NetScaler ADC (formerly Citrix ADC) and NetScaler Gateway (formerly Citrix Gateway) that could be exploited by an attacker to facilitate arbitrary file reads or trigger a denial-of-service (DoS) condition.

The vulnerabilities

items.title: China-Linked Group Targets Southeast Asia Critical Systems
items.summary: The group compromised at least 10 regional organizations, including two state-owned entities, and deployed a new backdoor.

items.title: Anthropic to restore Claude Fable access on Wednesday
items.summary: Anthropic has confirmed that the Department of Commerce has lifted export controls on Claude's two most powerful models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5. [...]

items.title: Anthropic rolls out Sonnet 5 with near-Opus 4.8 performance at a lower price
items.summary: Anthropic is now rolling out Sonnet 5, and it's almost as good as the Opus range, but it is designed to be cheaper than the company's flagship model. [...]

items.title: New BioShocking attack manipulates AI browser into data theft
items.summary: A new prompt injection attack dubbed "BioShocking" could trick AI-powered browsers into treating real-world risky actions as part of a fictional scenario, causing them to ignore any safety guardrails. [...]

items.title: Fake Bug Report Hijacks AI Coding Agents at Scale
items.summary: "Agentjacking" is the latest demonstration of how easily attackers can exploit an AI agent's inability to differentiate between content and instructions.

items.title: Microsoft accelerates quantum-safe roadmap as risks grow
items.summary: Microsoft announced today that it is accelerating its quantum-safe security roadmap, saying advances in quantum computing are bringing the need to replace today's encryption standards sooner than previously expected. [...]

items.title: Malicious PyPI packages give hackers control of Telegram bot servers
items.summary: A campaign active since last November has been targeting Python developers building Telegram bots with trojanized Pyrogram forks that allow attackers to read arbitrary files on compromised servers. [...]

items.title: Attackers Hijack Exposed AI Endpoints to Power Offensive Ops
items.summary: Attackers don't need any special authentication to reach a target endpoint — they just need to know where it is.

items.title: Why Identity Security Is Your Cyber Career Entry Point
items.summary: As AI reshapes cybersecurity workflows, John Paul Cunningham, CISO at SIlverfort, says the technology is creating opportunities rather than eliminating jobs — and there are more ways than ever to break into the essential field.

items.title: CIA chief highlights major shifts in agency’s tech approach
items.summary: CIA Director John Ratcliffe said artificial intelligence capabilities are "akin to digital nuclear weapons.”

items.title: Phishers Gain Persistence at EU, Asia Hospitality Orgs
items.summary: Separate but similar campaigns described by Microsoft and Trend Micro use malicious zip files to spread malware via social engineering and obsfucation, including blockchain abuse.

items.title: Microsoft Warns Poisoned MCP Tool Descriptions Can Make AI Agents Leak Data
items.summary:
New Microsoft research shows how attackers can hijack AI agents that act on a user's behalf, using nothing more than a poisoned tool description to make the agent quietly hand over company data to an outsider.

The trick is that the agent never breaks a rule. Every step looks routine, so i

items.title: RustDuck Botnet Rebuilds in Rust to Hijack Routers and Servers for DDoS
items.summary:
A new two-stage malware family called RustDuck is hijacking home routers, IP cameras, Android boxes, and poorly secured servers, then stitching them into a network built to knock websites and online services offline.

Researchers at QiAnXin's XLab have tracked it since February 2026, and s

items.title: House passes kids’ online safety bill, but Senate approval unlikely
items.summary: The Kids Internet and Digital Safety (KIDS) Act passed with bipartisan support by a 267-117 margin, winning the two-thirds majority needed to greenlight the legislation under a process that speeds up a bill’s path to a vote but requires more than a simple majority.

items.title: Nissan Discloses Employee Data Breach Linked to Oracle Zero-Day
items.summary: Nissan says employees' data was stolen via the Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day campaign

items.title: Langflow RCE Exploited to Deploy Monero Miner on Exposed AI App Endpoints
items.summary:
Threat actors are continuing to exploit a critical Langflow vulnerability as part of fresh attacks designed to deliver a Monero cryptocurrency miner.

The activity has been found to weaponize CVE-2026-33017 (CVSS score: 9.3), an unauthenticated remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in Langflow,

items.title: Fake Perplexity extension on Chrome Web Store tracked searches
items.summary: A malicious extension in the Chrome Web Store is masquerading as the Perplexity AI answer engine, intercepting search traffic and collecting browsing information. [...]

items.title: Critical SimpleHelp Vulnerability Exploited For Malware Delivery
items.summary: Attackers exploited a critical SimpleHelp RMM bug to deploy TaskWeaver and Djinn Stealer malware

items.title: BlueHammer Vulnerability Exploited in Ransomware Attacks
items.summary:
The Microsoft Defender vulnerability CVE-2026-33825 was exploited in the wild as a zero-day before patches were released.
The post BlueHammer Vulnerability Exploited in Ransomware Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: An intelligence budget 'super user' job is now in the hands of Russ Vought
